---
title: "Bioconductor: software for orchestrating high-throughput genomics data analysis"
author: |
  <br/>
  Sean Davis, MD, PhD<br/>
  Center for Cancer Research</br>
  National Cancer Institute</br>
  <a href="https://twitter.com/seandavis12">@seandavis12</a><br/>
  <a href="https://seandavi.github.io">https://seandavi.github.io</a>
date: "December 4, 2018"
output: 
  revealjs::revealjs_presentation:
    theme: night
    highlight: pygments
    center: false # do not center vertically
    reveal_options:
      slideNumber: true
      previewLinks: true
---
<style type="text/css">
  h1.title {
    text-align: left;
    font-style: italic;
  }
  .reveal .author,.reveal .date {
    text-align: left;
    text-size: 0.8em;
    margin-left: 100px;
  }
  p {
    text-align: left;
  }
  .reveal ul, ol {
    display: block;
    padding-left: 30px;
  }
  .reveal img {
     display: block;
     align: center;
  }
  blockquote p {
    text-align: center;
  }
  #left {
  left:-8.33%;
  text-align: left;
  float: left;
  width:50%;
  z-index:-10;
}

    #wrap { width: 1000px; height: 600px; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }
    #frame { width: 1000px; height: 600px; border: 1px solid black; }

#right {
  left:31.25%;
  top: 75px;
  float: right;
  text-align: right;
  z-index:-10;
  width:50%;
}
</style>

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE)
```

## Introduction

<div id="left">

![img](http://bioconductor.org/images/logo/jpg/bioconductor_logo_rgb.jpg)
![](https://s3.amazonaws.com/dev.assets.neo4j.com/wp-content/uploads/data-science-efficiency-neo4j-and-r.png)

</div>

<div id="right">
Analysis and comprehension of high-throughput biological data

- Started in 2002
- Builds on [R], which includes 14,376 "contributed" software packages and more than 2 million users worldwide

Bioconductor is well-used and well-respected

- More than 50,000 unique downloads per month
- Approximately 21,700 PubmedCentral citations

</div>

## But what *is* Bioconductor?

> Bioconductor is a Toolkit for high-throughput biological data science built with reproducible computational research as a *core value*.

Bioconductor software supports all aspects the biological data science workflow.

- Data import and export
- Data management and accessibility
- Data integration, including with public data
- Analysis and statistics
- High-level visualization
- Efficiency and reproducibility, even at scale

## Bioconductor Packages

```{r biocpkgdata, cache=TRUE}
require(dplyr)
require(yaml)
library(lubridate)
release_dates = yaml.load_file('https://bioconductor.org/config.yaml')$release_dates
releases = names(release_dates)
df = tibble(date = mdy(release_dates), releases=releases)
df = df[-c(1:8),]
df$packages = sapply(df$releases,function(v) {
  return(nrow(available.packages(repos = BiocInstaller::biocinstallRepos(version=as.character(v))['BioCsoft'])))
})
```

```{r biocpkgplot, fig.align="center"}
require(ggplot2)
ggplot(df,aes(x=date,y=packages)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method="glm",
             method.args=list(family=gaussian(link="log"))) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") + 
  theme(text = element_text(size=16))
```


## Bioconductor Infrastructure

<div id="wrap">
<iframe id="frame" src="https://bioconductor.org/"></iframe>
</div>

## Reusing code


```{r cars2, ref.label='cars', echo=FALSE}
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```



## SummarizedExperiment

<div style="width:80%; display: block; margin: 0 auto;">
![](https://images.nature.com/full/nature-assets/nmeth/journal/v12/n2/images/nmeth.3252-F2.jpg)
</div>

[R]: https://cran.r-project.org/
